dill: noun. Anethum graveolens (the type species of the genus Anethum), a herb, the seeds of which are moderately warming, pungent, and aromatic,
din: noun. A loud noise; a cacophony or loud commotion.
diol: noun. any organic compound having two hydroxy functional groups
dol: noun. The unit of measurement for pain.
doll: noun. A small figure resembling a human being that is used as a toy.
don: noun. A university professor, particularly one at Oxford or Cambridge.
down: adverb. From a higher position to a lower one; downwards.
hill: noun. An elevated landmass smaller than a mountain.
hin: noun. A former Hebrew liquid measure of volume (about 3.8 L).
hind: adjective. Located at the rear (most often said of animals' body parts).
hod: verb. To bob up and down on horseback, as an inexperienced rider may do; to jog.
hon: noun. A term of endearment; Honey, sweetheart
how: adverb. To what degree or extent.
howl: noun. The protracted, mournful cry of a dog, wolf or other canid; also of other animals.
idol: noun. A graven image or representation of anything that is revered, or believed to convey spiritual power.
indol: noun. Alternative form of indole used for derivatives starting with a vowel
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
lid: noun. The top or cover of a container.
lido: noun. Part of the sea by a beach sectioned off for swimming and other aquatic activities.
lin: verb. To desist, to stop, to cease.
lion: noun. A big cat, Panthera leo, native to Africa, India and formerly much of Europe.
loin: noun. The part of the body (of humans and quadrupeds) at each side of the backbone, between the ribs and hips.
low: adjective. Situated close to, or even below, the ground or another normal reference plane; not high or lofty.
nill: verb. To be unwilling; will not (+ infinitive).
nod: verb. To incline the head up and down, as to indicate agreement.
noh: noun. A form of classical Japanese music drama.
noil: noun. A short fibre left over from combing wool or spinning silk during the preparation of textile yarns.
old: adjective. Of an object, concept, relationship, etc., having existed for a relatively long period of time.
owl: noun. Any of various birds of prey of the order Strigiformes, families Strigidae and Tytonidae, that are primarily nocturnal and have
own: adjective. Belonging to; possessed; acquired and designated by; proper to; property of; titled to; held in one's name; under / using the
whid: noun. A quick motion; a rapid, quiet movement, usually by small game.
who: pronoun. What person or people; which person or people; asks for the identity of someone; used in a direct or indirect question.
will: verb. Used to express the future tense, sometimes with an implication of volition or determination when used in the first person.
wind: noun. Real or perceived movement of atmospheric air usually caused by convection or differences in air pressure.
wino: noun. A chronic or heavy drinker of cheap wine or other alcohol; especially, a drunkard.
wold: noun. An unforested or deforested plain, a grassland, a moor.
won: verb. simple past and past participle of win
dhol: noun. A type of drum from India.
dholl: noun. Archaic form of dal (“pigeon pea”).

hoi: interjection. Alternative form of hoy, hey ! a call to gain someone's attention.

ldl: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kaan.
lind: noun. The lime tree, or linden tree.
lld: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ladin.
loid: noun. A tool made from a piece of celluloid, used for bypassing locks.
loli: noun. A childlike female character, particularly in the lolicon genre.
nid: noun. Alternative form of nide (“nest of pheasants”).
nol: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomlaki.
noll: noun. The head, especially the top of the head.
oni: noun. A Japanese evil spirit or demon.
owd: verb. simple past and past participle of owe
